source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting, Shell Offshore Inc. GB 426-A

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Garden Banks Block 426

event.top_event: High-pressure hose release causing personnel injury
actions_taken: Production Operator closed all valves on the Boarding Valve Panel except BSDV 2471C to shut off pressure
Injured personnel transported to Platform Medic's office for evaluation
Injured personnel flown onshore for additional medical treatment including X-rays
BSEE investigation conducted with phone interviews and on-site inspection
event.summary: On 3 April 2021 at approximately 1310 hours, a contract chemical employee (IP) was assisting a Production Operator in testing a boarding shut down valve (BSDV) on the Cardamom Field at Shell's GB 426-A platform. While the IP was holding an unsecured hose used to drain residual fluid, the hose became pressurized when the equalizing valve BSDV 2471B was opened at the control panel. The high-pressure release caused the metal fitting on the hose to strike the IP's left hand and back, resulting in a closed displaced fracture of the fifth metacarpal bone. The IP was evacuated to an onshore medical facility for treatment.
recommendations: BSEE Lafayette District made no recommendations to the Regional Office of Incident Investigations

hazards.name: Boarding shut down valve testing operations

threats.name: Unexpected pressurization of drain hose during BSDV testing
Equalizing valve opened during blowdown procedure
Inexperienced personnel assigned to BSDV testing task

consequences.name: Personnel struck by pressurized hose fitting
Closed displaced fracture of fifth metacarpal bone
Medical evacuation and onshore treatment required

controls.name: Approved Operating Procedure for BSDV testing
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to prevent deviation from safe work practices
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control establishing correct blowdown procedure

supporting_text: The Operating Procedure states that blowing down to 'zero pressure is not required. Blowdown to a point where at least 500 psi differential is across the boarding valve and monitor for pressure build-up'
Utilizing the referenced hose (to achieve zero pressure) was not part of the approved operating procedure

According to the Shell Auger OP - BV Check for the Cardamom Field, there is no mention of using a hose throughout the testing procedure. All piping mentioned throughout the bleed down and testing procedure is hard piping.

controls.name: Personnel competency and experience verification
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure only qualified personnel perform BSDV testing
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring personnel experience for task assignment

supporting_text: The IP had no experience with testing the BSDV's and did not realize that the hose could pressurize
BSEE also found that the probable cause of the injury was allowing the inexperienced IP to assist in the testing of the BSDV regarding the referenced operation(s)

controls.name: Hose securing and restraint measures
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent hose movement during pressurization
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to physically restrain unsecured hose

supporting_text: A hose that was not secured was installed between BSDVs 2471A and 2471B
A hose that was not secured, was installed between the 2471A and 2471B valves which is downstream of the BSDV

controls.name: Pressure isolation and lock-out/tag-out procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unexpected pressurization
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to isolate pressure sources during maintenance

supporting_text: Shell was unable to identify who opened the BSDV 2471B equalizing valve while completing the blowing down of the flowlines
This occurred due to the equalizing valve BSDV 2471B being opened at the LCP-2470 Cardamom Boarding Valve Control Panel

controls.name: Supervision and work coordination
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure safe work practices and communication
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring supervisor oversight of operations

supporting_text: There were three control room operators monitoring the pressures of the BSDV's during the testing
The Production Operator reviewed the procedure with the IP
The Production Operator proceeded to the panel and closed all valves on the Boarding Valve Panel except for the BSDV 2471C, to shut off the pressure to the hose

controls.name: Emergency response and medical evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Mitigation control to minimize injury consequences
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control for medical response and evacuation

supporting_text: The IP was taken to the Platform Medic's office to be evaluated
The Platform Medic consulted with an Onshore Physician and the decision was made to fly the IP onshore for additional treatment
The IP was transported to a medical facility for additional medical treatment including X-rays

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
